[~Wi'I1J-~4] NATIONAL COUNCIL FOR TEACHER EDUCATION NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 28th December, 2005 F. No. 49-2912005-NCTE (N & S).- Regulations laying down norms and standards for various teacher education programmes promulgated by the National Council for Teacher Education provide for, inter-alia, the eligibility criteria for appointment to teaching posts for various teacher education programmes. For appointment to the post of lecturer concerning degree level teacher education programmes, NCTE regulations also. include other qualifications as may be prescribed by regulatory bodies like the University Grants Commission. Due to acute shortage of candidates fully eligible for such appointments, -several representations have been received from degree level teacher training institutions for relaxation of the requirement relating to passing of NET/ SLET. In order to make available adequate number of candidates for appointment to various teaching posts in teacher education institutions, the National Council for Teacher Education has decided to grant certain relaxation in this regard without compromising the quality. Now, therefore, in exercise of poviers conferred under Section 32 (2)(d)(i) of the NCTE Act,1993( 73 of 1993), the National Council for Teacher Education hereby makes the following regulations, namely :- 1. Short Title and Commencement (1) These regulations may be called "The National Council for Teacher Education (the criteria for relaxation in qualifications requir~d for appointment to teaching posts in degree level teacher training courses) Regulations, 2005. (2) They shall come into force from the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Relaxation of Qualification for appointment to teaching posts (1) Normally no person shall be appoi.nted to a teaching post for a degree level teacher training course recognised by the National Council for Teacher Education, i.e., REd., M.Ed., B.P.Ed. and M.P.Ed. courses, if he/she does not fulfil the 5 ! I i II,i I i I I r -- 5 of 8 -- . ' 6 THE GAZEITE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PARTHI-SEC. 4J (2) (3) (4) (5) (6) . I minimum qualifi<;:ations prescribed in the I norms .. and standards laid down by the National Councill for Teacher Education for the particular course. : I However in the event of non-availability of ade4uate number , . r. of suitable candidates, on the request of the applIcant institution, grant of relaxation only with regard to passing of NET/SLET can be considered by the NCTE subject to the following conditions:- . I a) The availability of vacancies was properly advertised in the National/Local Dailies or Roigar Samachar giving full details of pay scale, duties etc. to ~ttract eligible . candidates. I b) All. the candidates eligible under normal conditions available for appointment have been appointed and some posts still remain vacant. I c) The relaxation would be considered only as a temporary measure for a period of 3 ye:ars permitting regular appointment subject to confirmation after the appointee becomes eligible on clearance of I NETJSLET jPh.D. etc. I Relaxation of the requirement of NET/SLET: can be made only by the competent authority in the ,NatiOI~alCouncil for Teacher Education. . . . :. The request for relaxation would be considered by the said authority taking into account sound justific~tion based on the recommendation of the duly constitb.ted Selection Committee which considered the ..available i:tpplicants. A committee would be considered duly constituted only if at least one expert nominated by the concerned examining body jaffiliating university j organisation is represented therein~i . An institutionj organisation shall submit to the NCTE Hqrs its proposal s.eeking such relaxation with regard to passing of NET/ SLET in the prescribed format immediately after selection of the eligible candidates. . I Such cases shall be considered by the committee(s) of at least three experts~ constituted by -the Chairperson of the National Council for Teacher Education. IThe committee shall take into account all the relevant factors including, inter alia, the comments of the Selection Co~mittee and the documents furnished by the institutio~, for: recommending grant of relaxation or otherwise. I . r I -- 6 of 8 -- •• . , ~;~I1I-~4] : . (7) The expert committee shall submit its findings for approval by the Chairperson. (8) Letter of appointment to the selected candidates proposed to be appointed on relaxed conditions shall be issued by the institution concerned only after receipt of relaxation order from the National Council' for Teacher Education. (9) In respect of teachers already working and requiring exemption from NET / SLET before issuance of this notification, proposals for seeking rela.'{ation from the requirement of NET/SLET shan be submitted to the headquarters of the National Council for Teacher Education on the prescribed format along with the approval of the competent/ appointing authority, within two months of the publication of the notification. (10) The candidates so appointed under the relaxed standards, at the earliest possible opportunity shan register themselves for Ph.D. and complete their Ph.D. within five years of their appointment. The institution shan furnish to the National Council for Teacher Education proof of registration of the candidate for Ph. D. within one year from his/her appointment. (11) Failure to comply with the provisions of these regulations may render the continuation of appointment 'On relaxed standards void. L. R. AGGARWAL, Dy. Secy. [ADVT.
